codes.term: Recurrent hepatitis C virus induced liver disease following liver transplant
codes.rationale: This represents recurrent hepatitis C after transplant, which is a post-transplant complication rather than primary chronic hepatitis C.

codes.term: Viral hepatitis C in mother during pregnancy
codes.rationale: Viral hepatitis C in pregnancy is a contextual variant; the query specifies primary chronic hepatitis C without pregnancy context.

codes.term: Reactivation of hepatitis C viral hepatitis
codes.rationale: Reactivation of hepatitis C is a specific clinical event distinct from chronic hepatitis C itself and represents a change in disease status rather than the primary condition.

codes.term: Cirrhosis of liver due to chronic hepatitis C
codes.rationale: Cirrhosis of liver due to chronic hepatitis C is a complication of chronic hepatitis C, not an instance of chronic hepatitis C itself; the term does not name the primary condition.

codes.term: Cryoglobulinemia due to chronic hepatitis C
codes.rationale: Cryoglobulinemia is a complication of chronic hepatitis C; the term does not name the queried condition itself.

codes.term: Hepatic coma due to chronic hepatitis C
codes.rationale: Hepatic coma due to chronic hepatitis C is a severe complication, not an instance of the primary condition itself.

summary: Recall: 70.0% (14 of 20 reference codes retrieved). Precision: 51.8%. F1: 59.6%. 6 reference codes were incorrectly excluded.

scored_codes.rationale: This represents recurrent hepatitis C after transplant, which is a post-transplant complication rather than primary chronic hepatitis C.

scored_codes.term: Viral hepatitis C in mother during pregnancy
scored_codes.rationale: Viral hepatitis C in pregnancy is a contextual variant; the query specifies primary chronic hepatitis C without pregnancy context.

scored_codes.rationale: Reactivation of hepatitis C is a specific clinical event distinct from chronic hepatitis C itself and represents a change in disease status rather than the primary condition.
